submit_method: CDP (Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222). Opened a fresh kimi.com tab (0B7016C8C57DBCDC6C523F389881DA10) via /json/new, activated it and forced the Chrome window (PID 7684) to OS-foreground via Win32 ShowWindow+SetForegroundWindow so CDP synthetic Input is not silently dropped. Hard-reloaded twice (Page.reload ignoreCache) per the kimi hydration note: after the second reload the SPA was fully hydrated (avatar present, send-button-container present, div.chat-input-editor natively contenteditable='true'). AUTH NOTE: this session was ANONYMOUS — localStorage carried anonymous_access_token / anonymous_refresh_token (no logged-in 최장희 token), and the sidebar showed 'Log In' / 'Log in to sync chat history'. Kimi permits anonymous chat (avatar shown, editor editable, send fires a Chat POST), so the engine path is still exercised; the APF interception is login-independent and rendered the warning byte-identically to logged-in baselines. Prompt delivered via Input.insertText for clean Hangul (editor innerText length 24, matches '내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567'); send-button-container became enabled (no 'disabled' class). Send-button center read live from getBoundingClientRect = (869.6,367.6). Winning channel reproduced from #1227/#1248/#1291/#1313/#1334: fire the in-flight kimi Chat request (trusted Input.dispatchMouseEvent click at send-button center + Enter rawKeyDown/char(\r)/keyUp) then Page.reload ignoreCache while the PII prompt request is in flight; the Etap APF engine intercepted the re-issued request (resident registration number 901010-1234567) and replaced the whole response document with its block-warning HTML.

warning_evidence.title: 보안 경고 - 정책 - 접근 차단
warning_evidence.body_snippet: ⚠️ 보안 경고\n민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\n(This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)

regression_vs_baseline: MATCH (PASS -> PASS, stable). kimi reproduces the W1-W5.3 baselines BYTE-IDENTICALLY: div#apf-warning-banner present, document_html_len exactly 853, '차단' + '보안 경고' present, editor gone, engine_intercept_fired=true. The W5.4 change migrated the LAST WS builder — character (build_character_add_turn_envelope -> DB-driven emit_ws_envelope) — to the DB-driven path; kimi is a non-character, non-WS service and is unaffected, confirming it stays IDENTICAL to the W5.2 (#1334) / W5.3 baseline as expected for the 20 non-character IDs. Only delta vs #1334 is the auth state (this run anonymous vs #1334 logged-in 최장희), which did NOT change the rendered output — the engine doc replacement is identical (html_len 853, same banner, same title code points), reinforcing that APF interception is independent of kimi login state.
notes: W5.4 character-migration regression re-run of kimi check-warning. Setup: fresh CDP tab on existing Chrome (port 9222), Chrome forced OS-foreground. Hydration: first hard reload already showed editor editable + avatar; a second hard reload was performed per the standing note and confirmed full hydration (editor contenteditable='true', send enables after Input.insertText). Session was ANONYMOUS (anonymous_access_token present) but kimi allows anonymous chat, so the Chat POST fired and the engine intercepted it. Post-reload DOM scan: 차단 found, 경고/보안 found, document.title decodes to '보안 경고 - 정책 - 접근 차단', div#apf-warning-banner present, full document HTML length 853 (static block page, not the kimi SPA), editor gone. verdict=PASS_WARNING_RENDERED, engine_intercept_fired=true, warning_rendered=true. Screenshot: 1375_warning (APF '차단' block document).
status_detail: PASS_WARNING_RENDERED — engine replaced doc with apf-warning-banner (차단), html_len 853, byte-identical to W1-W5.3 baseline; anonymous session, engine path login-independent
